heading: Destruction or loss of, or injury to, sanctuary resources
label: 1443 Destruction or loss of, or injury to, sanctuary resources
notes: References in TextSections 4281–4289 of the Revised Statutes of the United States, referred to in subsec. (a)(4), were classified to sections 181 to 188 of the former Appendix to Title 46, Shipping, and section 175 of former Title 46. Sections 4281 to 4287 and 4289 of the Revised Statutes were repealed and restated in chapter 305 of Title 46, Shipping, by , §§ 6(c), 19, , , 1710. children.label: A
children.text: the amount of response costs and damages resulting from the destruction, loss, or injury; and

children.label: B
children.text: interest on that amount calculated in the manner described under .section 2705 of title 33

children.heading: Liability in rem
children.label: 2 Liability in rem
children.text: Any vessel used to destroy, cause the loss of, or injure any sanctuary resource shall be liable in rem to the United States for response costs and damages resulting from such destruction, loss, or injury. The amount of that liability shall constitute a maritime lien on the vessel and may be recovered in an action in rem in any district court of the United States that has jurisdiction over the vessel.

children.label: A
children.text: the destruction or loss of, or injury to, the sanctuary resource was caused solely by an act of God, an act of war, or an act or omission of a third party, and the person acted with due care;

children.label: B
children.text: the destruction, loss, or injury was caused by an activity authorized by Federal or State law; or

children.label: C
children.text: the destruction, loss, or injury was negligible.

children.heading: Limits to liability
children.label: 4 Limits to liability
children.text: Nothing in sections 4281–4289 of the Revised Statutes of the United States or shall limit the liability of any person under this chapter.section 30706 of title 46

children.heading: Response actions and damage assessment
children.label: b Response actions and damage assessment

children.heading: Response actions
children.label: 1 Response actions
children.text: The Secretary may undertake or authorize all necessary actions to prevent or minimize the destruction or loss of, or injury to, sanctuary resources, or to minimize the imminent risk of such destruction, loss, or injury.

children.heading: Damage assessment
children.label: 2 Damage assessment
children.text: The Secretary shall assess damages to sanctuary resources in accordance with .section 1432(6) of this title

children.heading: Civil actions for response costs and damages
children.label: c Civil actions for response costs and damages

children.text: The Attorney General, upon request of the Secretary, may commence a civil action against any person or vessel who may be liable under subsection (a) for response costs and damages. The Secretary, acting as trustee for sanctuary resources for the United States, shall submit a request for such an action to the Attorney General whenever a person may be liable for such costs or damages.

children.label: A
children.text: the defendant is located, resides, or is doing business, in the case of an action against a person;

children.label: B
children.text: the vessel is located, in the case of an action against a vessel; or

children.label: C
children.text: the destruction of, loss of, or injury to a sanctuary resource occurred.

children.label: A
children.text: to reimburse the Secretary or any other Federal or State agency that conducted those activities; and

children.label: B
children.text: after reimbursement of such costs, to restore, replace, or acquire the equivalent of any sanctuary resource.

children.label: A
children.text: to restore, replace, or acquire the equivalent of the sanctuary resources that were the subject of the action, including for costs of monitoring and the costs of curation and conservation of archeological, historical, and cultural sanctuary resources;

children.label: B
children.text: to restore degraded sanctuary resources of the national marine sanctuary that was the subject of the action, giving priority to sanctuary resources and habitats that are comparable to the sanctuary resources that were the subject of the action; and

children.label: C
children.text: to restore degraded sanctuary resources of other national marine sanctuaries.

children.heading: Federal-State coordination
children.label: 3 Federal-State coordination
children.text: Amounts recovered under this section with respect to sanctuary resources lying within the jurisdiction of a State shall be used under paragraphs (2)(A) and (B) in accordance with the court decree or settlement agreement and an agreement entered into by the Secretary and the Governor of that State.

children.heading: Statute of limitations
children.label: e Statute of limitations
children.text: An action for response costs or damages under subsection (c) shall be barred unless the complaint is filed within 3 years after the date on which the Secretary completes a damage assessment and restoration plan for the sanctuary resources to which the action relates.
